@for (message of messages; track message; let i = $index) {
{{ getContact(message.senderId)?.name || 'Unknown' }} @if (message.isDeleted) { [Message deleted] } @else { {{ getMessageContent(message.content) }} } @if (hasReactions(message)) {
@for (reaction of getReactionsByEmoji(message); track reaction.emoji) { }
} @if (showEmojiPicker[message.id]) {
@for (emoji of commonEmojis; track emoji) { }
}
@if (message.isEdited && !message.isDeleted) { edited } {{ message.timestamp | date : 'shortTime' }} @if (isSent(message.senderId) && !message.isDeleted) { }
} @if (typingUsers.length > 0) {
{{ typingUsers.length === 1 ? getContactName(typingUsers[0]) : typingUsers.length + ' people' }} {{ typingUsers.length === 1 ? 'is' : 'are' }} typing...
}

variables.name: SOCKET_HOST
variables.description:
Injection Tokens for the SocketChatService. These tokens allow for configuration of the socket connection, such as the host URL and namespace. They can be provided in the app module or any other module to customize the socket connection settings.

SOCKET_HOST: The base URL for the socket server. SOCKET_NAMESPACE: The namespace for the socket connection. SOCKET_IO_INSTANCE: The instance of the socket.io client library. SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN_PROVIDER: Function that returns the current auth token. SOCKET_AUTH_ERROR_HANDLER: Function called when auth errors occur.

These tokens should be set by YOU when providing the service in your application. For example:

Example :
providers: [
  {
    provide: SOCKET_HOST,
    useValue: 'http://your-socket-server.com'
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_NAMESPACE,
   useValue: 'your-namespace'
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_IO_INSTANCE,
    useValue: io
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN_PROVIDER,
    useFactory: (authService) => () => authService.getToken(),
    deps: [AuthService]
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_AUTH_ERROR_HANDLER,
    useFactory: (router, authService) => () => {
      authService.logout();
      router.navigate(['/login']);
    },
    deps: [Router, AuthService]
 }]

functions.name: constructConversation
functions.description: Constructs a conversation object with filtered contacts and last message.

functions.name: filterContactsByConversation
functions.description: Filters contacts to only those participating in the conversation.

libs/chat-ui/src/lib/socket-chat.service.name: SOCKET_HOST
libs/chat-ui/src/lib/socket-chat.service.description:
Injection Tokens for the SocketChatService. These tokens allow for configuration of the socket connection, such as the host URL and namespace. They can be provided in the app module or any other module to customize the socket connection settings.

SOCKET_HOST: The base URL for the socket server. SOCKET_NAMESPACE: The namespace for the socket connection. SOCKET_IO_INSTANCE: The instance of the socket.io client library. SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN_PROVIDER: Function that returns the current auth token. SOCKET_AUTH_ERROR_HANDLER: Function called when auth errors occur.

These tokens should be set by YOU when providing the service in your application. For example:

Example :
providers: [
  {
    provide: SOCKET_HOST,
    useValue: 'http://your-socket-server.com'
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_NAMESPACE,
   useValue: 'your-namespace'
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_IO_INSTANCE,
    useValue: io
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN_PROVIDER,
    useFactory: (authService) => () => authService.getToken(),
    deps: [AuthService]
 },{
    provide: SOCKET_AUTH_ERROR_HANDLER,
    useFactory: (router, authService) => () => {
      authService.logout();
      router.navigate(['/login']);
    },
    deps: [Router, AuthService]
 }]
